AC: 5
WIS: +7
SV COLD: +10
WT: 1.5 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG BER
Race: ELF HIE DEF DWF HFL GNM FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

This belt
QuoteReply
# Oct 22 2001 at 5:24 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762604528
9 posts
I got extremely lucky in EC the other day. A druid had unwittingly purchased this not thinking to ask or check if this is something that she should use. She then sent a shout saying that she was looking to sell this for a mere 450pp. I couldn't believe my luck...The lowest I have ever seen this sell for on Terris-thule is 4kpp...I jumped at the opportunity. I am throughouly enjoying the upgrade to my crystalline belt :)

Akarana Healingwind
53 cleric of Tunare
Dwarec Mercs
Terris-Thule
▲ Page top
Drops
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2001 at 4:17 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763464591
16 posts
FYI, tonite, a fellow druid and I hunted the cycle in FM for a bit. Out of 5 cycles, meaning 5 chances of spawn, the Burynai grand cenobite spawned twice. He dropped the belt both times. Just my observations. However, out of all the cycles I have done, which isn't many, around 15 or so, this bad boy has popped just those two times. The key to him is nuke nuke, and then keep nuking. ) If anyone knows the relative price on this belt or knows if Alak is right or close enough with the pricing, I would be curious to what they are going for. Thanx in advance. ) Send me a tell in game on Seventh Hammer if you want to buy, we can work something out. )

Kistanilay Elessar
Karana's Faithful
▲ Page top
BELT OF CENOBITE CHANGE, AUGUST 31, 2001
QuoteReply
# Aug 31 2001 at 7:33 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965962087
1 post
Recently we made an error with a change to the Belt of the Cenobite. A player pointed out that there was an error with the belt, that it was usable by Monks but not by humans. So we changed it to make it usable by humans. However, the belt is a small belt, intended only for the use of small races. The error on the item was not that it was unusable by humans, but that Monk was listed as a class that could use it.
So with the next patch we'll be removing both humans and Monks from the list of folks that can use this item. We just wanted you to know.


plz note that this is copy past from http://eq.castersrealm.com/
▲ Page top
RE: BELT OF CENOBITE CHANGE, AUGUST 31, 2001
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2002 at 12:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759046203

I really dont get that... why not remove monks from the list in the first place? Verant seems a bit umm "confused" sometimes..

10 posts

Burynai Grand Cenobite
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2001 at 5:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750155267
View User Forum
19 posts
Dropped by Burynai Grand Cenobite in north west corner of zone. He is very rare. I've done the rares there for quite a while and only seen him once while I've seen the other ultra rares several times. Very hard kill for me (42 Bard), had to let him run out of mana nukeing me before he dropped. Belt is amazing... worth the near death experience. =)

help
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2001 at 11:50 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756952173
4 posts
i have been trying for weeks to get info on this darn belt and how to get it, im to the point of total frustration... does anyone know the PH for the grand cenobite, where he spawns, spawn time...anything to help me get this item? i would appreciate any further help
▲ Page top
Spawns in NW Corner of Zone
QuoteReply
# Apr 03 2001 at 9:35 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591756729570
9 posts
The Grand Cenobite Pops in the northwest corner of the Frontier Mountain Zone. He has several placeholders. These include the basic Burynai Miner, Gemeye, Eboneye, and various Named Sarnacks. Apparently the Burynai Miner and the Named Burynai in this area are the common spawn. Then the Named Sarnacks, the uncommon spawn and finally the Grand Cenobite is Ultra rare here. I grouped and hunted this area continuously for 2 weeks for many hours at a time killing the named Goblins,Giants,Burynai and Sarnacks. Having a tracker is a MUST since as soon as you kill one rare another pops someplace else in zone. Usually going between the Northwest Corner and Southeast corner I would eventually find a rare on track. If you kill one in SE area head to within hailing distance of the South wall and run West you usually will pick up another rare on tracking. If it is a giant or a goblin kill it and continue NW and see if you pick up a Burynai or Sarnack rare. If not go back to SE corner and start again. If you don't track a rare there kill all the Goblin Raiders and a rare will usually pop somewhere close by. If not go back to the NW corner and kill the Burynai Miners there. BE CAREFUL in the NW zone. There are ALOT of wandering mobs that are VERY aggressive. After Killing all the Burynai Miners there a Rare should pop or another Burynai Miner. Killing that Burynai miner should spawn a rare for sure. This system seemed to work well for me. In one night I got the Dusty Platemail, Belt of the Cenobite(was Lucky), Dustscryers Crystal Ball, Gemfinder, and Cheiftain's Headress. The rares did not always drop the nice item. Either was item or just Money with nothing else. The giants would drop all kinds of stuff and was pretty random. Most of these mobs were blue to a level 30ish group but several are yellow or red. A good solid group of four seems to work best at these levels but you definately need a tracker, Nuker, Snare and root or Chanter for mob control.Good Luck in your hunting.

Lvis
Bard, Terris Thule
▲ Page top
dropped by.....
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2001 at 11:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753903927
1 post
This item is dropped by the Burynia Grand Cenobite...spawns up near the cave entrance to Droga...He is approx lvl 35-40 and does cast superior healing on himself.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Dec 22 2000 at 11:28 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) What Mob drops this?
RE: what drops?
QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2000 at 9:06 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593965819311
11 posts
It's dropped by the Burynai Grand Cenobite. I'd guess he's about mid 30s, but he's a cleric and likes using his nukes (which interrupt your casting) and heals himself a lot (if given the chance), so have someone slamming, bashing or stunning him when you take him down.

I've killed him 4 times and he dropped the belt every time.

Giliad Stormdancer
L50 wood elf druid
Rodcet Nife server
▲ Page top
